finding: Event Timing coverage evidence must carry the same opaque navigation identity as replay evidence before either can authorize interpretation.

primary_sources.relevance: Navigation timing is defined for the current document, uses the document time origin, and exposes one navigation entry in the performance timeline.
Event Timing observations are performance entries produced within a document performance timeline; entry presence alone does not establish cross-navigation identity.

evidence_strength: repository-corroborated prototype with deterministic unit tests

verification.fresh_checkout_attempt: blocked because the execution container could not resolve github.com
verification.integrated_suite_status: committed but not independently executed in this run

uncertainty: The cited W3C documents are Working Drafts and may change.
No physical Safari packet was evaluated.
The integrated gate suite was not independently executed after commit.
falsification_route: Provide conforming evidence showing coverage can be safely reused across distinct document navigations without explicit identity binding.
Show a same-navigation packet rejected by the token and equality rules.
Run the integrated suite and demonstrate a regression caused by the new binding layer.
